curriculum.sourceVerificationNote: Academic lead must supply verified CP identifiers before curriculum-alignment claims.

competencies.title: Familiar spoken interaction
competencies.description: Interact in everyday school situations: introductions, small talk, preferences, invitations, clarification.

competencies.title: Everyday listening comprehension
competencies.description: Understand announcements and informal conversations for gist and detail.

competencies.title: Descriptive texts
competencies.description: Comprehend and produce descriptions of people and places.

competencies.title: Recount texts
competencies.description: Comprehend and produce recounts of past events.

competencies.title: Narrative basics
competencies.description: Tell a simple connected story with sequencing language.

competencies.title: Procedure texts
competencies.description: Explain and follow simple sequenced procedures.

competencies.title: Functional reading and writing
competencies.description: Use posters, forms, and short messages for real purposes.

competencies.title: Language awareness in context
competencies.description: Use present and past forms, prepositions of place, and question forms to support communication.

objectives.title: Introduce yourself and respond naturally in a new-school context
Open, maintain, and close small talk
Understand school announcements for gist and detail
Make, accept, and decline invitations politely
Recount past events with past simple
Ask for help, repetition, and clarification
Explain a simple procedure in sequence
Write short messages with appropriate informal register

assets.purpose: Original campus corridor scene placeholder pending production art
Original cafeteria scene placeholder pending production art
Original assembly hall scene placeholder pending production art
Original Bridge Club room scene placeholder pending production art
Original library scene placeholder pending production art
Original SMK media workshop scene placeholder pending production art

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Hari pertama di Kampus Terpadu Cakrawala. Pemain bertemu Hana di gerbang dan menjalani perkenalan pertama yang menentukan kesan awal.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: Survive Day One — perkenalkan diri, lanjutkan obrolan ringan, dan temukan kelas Anda.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Pilihan alami/hangat menaikkan kepercayaan Hana; pilihan datar tidak menghukum, hanya membedakan reaksi.

criteria.descriptorId: Ungkapan yang dipilih sesuai dengan sumber belajar yang direferensikan.
Ungkapan sesuai dengan lawan bicara, tujuan, dan situasi baru.
Pilihan mempertahankan makna dan register yang ditargetkan.

dialogueNodes.text: Hey, you look new. First day at Cakrawala?

choices.text: Yeah, it is. I'm still getting used to everything, but I'm excited to be here.

feedback.message: Terbuka dan alami — Anda memberi Hana detail yang mudah ditanggapi.
feedback.modelUtterance: I'm still getting used to everything, but I'm excited to be here.

choices.text: Yes. I am pleased to make your acquaintance on this first school day.

feedback.message: Tata bahasanya benar, tetapi register terlalu formal untuk teman sebaya di gerbang sekolah.
feedback.modelUtterance: Yeah, it's my first day. Nice to meet you.

choices.text: Maybe. I don't know. It's just a school, I guess.

feedback.message: Hana memahami Anda, tetapi jawaban mengambang tidak memberi bahan percakapan.
feedback.modelUtterance: Yeah, it's my first day. I'm a little nervous, actually.

dialogueNodes.text: You'll get used to it, trust me. I'm Hana, by the way — 10-B, same as you, I saw the list. So what are you into?
Wow, very official! Relax, we're the same grade. I'm Hana. So — what are you into?
Fair enough, first days are weird. I'm Hana. Let's try again — what are you into?
Come on, anything. Games, food, music. I promise I won't judge. Much.

choices.text: I'm really into music, actually. Mostly stuff to survive homework. How about you?

feedback.message: Sempurna: berbagi detail lalu bertanya balik. Pola "I'm really into… How about you?" menjaga percakapan hidup.
feedback.modelUtterance: I'm really into music. How about you?

choices.text: Music.

feedback.message: Satu kata mematikan percakapan — tidak salah, tetapi tidak memberi apa pun untuk ditanggapi dan tidak bertanya balik.
feedback.modelUtterance: I'm really into music. How about you?

choices.text: I am hobby of music.

feedback.message: "Hobby" adalah kata benda, bukan kata kerja. Gunakan "My hobby is music" atau lebih alami: "I'm really into music."
feedback.modelUtterance: I'm really into music.

dialogueNodes.text: Ha! Same. Okay, I officially approve of you. Come on, the bell's about to ring — I'll walk you in.
…Music. Great. Detailed. Okay, mystery person, let's walk — the bell's about to ring.
Music! Got it. You can just say you're into music, by the way. Come on, the bell's about to ring.
Welcome to Cakrawala. Stick with me and you'll survive week one. Probably.

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Tersesat di koridor Cakrawala, pemain bertemu Ryo — yang memberi arah akurat tetapi sangat singkat. Pemain harus berani meminta klarifikasi.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: Find Room 10-B before the second bell — minta arah, klarifikasi, dan konfirmasi.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Ryo menghargai pertanyaan yang efisien dan konfirmasi yang tepat; dia tidak menyukai basa-basi berlebihan.

dialogueNodes.text: Two identical corridors. Zero signs that make sense. The second bell rings in four minutes. A student in a workshop apron walks past, carrying a tripod.
He doesn't look chatty. But he clearly knows where he's going.

choices.text: Excuse me — how do I get to Room 10-B? I'm kind of lost.

feedback.message: "Excuse me" + pertanyaan langsung: sopan, efisien, tepat untuk orang asing yang sedang buru-buru.
feedback.modelUtterance: Excuse me, how do I get to Room 10-B?

choices.text: Hey. 10-B. Where?

feedback.message: Dia tetap menjawab — tetapi Anda terdengar memerintah orang asing. Satu "excuse me" mengubah segalanya.
feedback.modelUtterance: Excuse me, how do I get to Room 10-B?

choices.text: Good morning, I sincerely apologize for this interruption to your most important activities…

feedback.message: Terlalu panjang untuk situasi empat-menit-sebelum-bel. Konteks menentukan panjang pembukaan.
feedback.modelUtterance: Excuse me — Room 10-B?

dialogueNodes.text: Second corridor. Past the labs. Right at the stairs. Third door left.
…Second corridor. Past the labs. Right at the stairs. Third door left. You're welcome.
Skip the speech. Second corridor, past the labs, right at the stairs, third door left. Four minutes.
That was… fast. Right at the stairs? Or was it left? He's already turning to go.

choices.text: Sorry — so you mean right at the stairs, then third door on the left, right?

feedback.message: Konfirmasi dengan parafrase — alat dari pelajaran klarifikasi, dipakai tepat waktu. Ryo justru menghargai ini.
feedback.modelUtterance: So you mean right at the stairs, then third door on the left, right?

choices.text: (Say nothing and hope for the best.)

feedback.message: Diam terasa aman — sampai Anda berdiri di depan ruang guru yang salah. Bertanya lebih murah daripada tersesat.
feedback.modelUtterance: Sorry, could you say that again?

choices.text: Wait, could you say that again? A bit slower?

feedback.message: Meminta pengulangan itu strategi, bukan kelemahan. Perhatikan: dunia tidak runtuh.
feedback.modelUtterance: Could you say that again, a bit slower?

dialogueNodes.text: Correct. You listen. Good. Most people just nod and get lost.
Second corridor. Past the labs. Right at the stairs. Third door on the left. Slower enough?

You turn left at the stairs. The door you open is definitely not 10-B — twenty seniors turn to stare. You close it very, very slowly, walk back, and take the RIGHT turn this time. Lesson learned the loud way.
Third door on the left: 10-B. Thirty seconds to spare. The apron guy is gone — but you'll see him again.
You slide into 10-B just as the bell dies. Next time — confirm the directions. It costs five seconds.

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Kantin di jam sibuk. Hana mengundang pemain bergabung; Ryo duduk sendirian. Pilihan sosial pertama yang berdampak pada dua hubungan.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: Lunch Politics — tanggapi undangan, nyatakan selera, dan putuskan di mana duduk.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Mengajak Ryo bergabung membuka jalur persahabatan lebih awal. Menolak Hana dengan pelembut tidak merusak hubungan; menolak datar iya.

dialogueNodes.text: There you are! We grabbed the corner table. Want to join us? Fair warning — Rina will interrogate you about your entire life.

choices.text: Sure, count me in. I'll risk the interrogation.

feedback.message: "Count me in" + humor balasan — penerimaan yang hidup, bukan sekadar "yes".
feedback.modelUtterance: Sure, count me in.

choices.text: I'd love to, but I promised to call my mom at lunch. Rain check?

feedback.message: Penolakan tiga bagian: apresiasi + alasan + harapan. Hana tersenyum, hubungan aman.
feedback.modelUtterance: I'd love to, but I've promised something. Rain check?

choices.text: No.

feedback.message: Tata bahasa: sempurna. Efek sosial: Hana mengira Anda tidak menyukainya. Penolakan butuh pelembut.
feedback.modelUtterance: I'd love to, but I can't today. Maybe tomorrow?

dialogueNodes.text: Deal — tomorrow you're ours. Tell your mom the corner table says hi.
Oh. Okay. Sure. …See you around, then.
Okay, big question before you sit: bakso or mie ayam? This table takes food very seriously.
Well? Choose wisely. Friendships have ended over this.

choices.text: Honestly? I'd rather have bakso — but I'm not really a fan of the queue.

feedback.message: "I'd rather" + "not really a fan of" — dua pola preferensi dipakai sekaligus, dengan humor.
feedback.modelUtterance: I'd rather have bakso, but I'm not really a fan of the queue.

choices.text: Mie ayam. Bakso is disgusting.

feedback.message: Di meja penggemar bakso, "disgusting" terlalu keras. "I'm not really into bakso" menyampaikan hal yang sama tanpa menginjak selera orang.
feedback.modelUtterance: I'm not really into bakso, honestly.

choices.text: I am liking mie ayam since I am child.

feedback.message: Kata kerja rasa (like) jarang memakai bentuk -ing, dan "since I was a child" butuh lampau: "I've liked mie ayam since I was a kid."
feedback.modelUtterance: I've liked mie ayam since I was a kid.

dialogueNodes.text: Across the room, the guy from the corridor — Ryo — eats alone, reading a camera manual like it's a thriller.

choices.text: Walk over: "Hey — corridor guy. Want to join us? There's space."

feedback.message: Undangan santai tanpa tekanan. "There's space" memberi Ryo jalan keluar yang mudah — itu justru membuatnya nyaman.
feedback.modelUtterance: Want to join us? There's space.

choices.text: (He probably prefers to be alone. Leave it.)

feedback.message: Pilihan cerita yang sah — beberapa orang memang butuh ruang. Kesempatan lain akan datang.
feedback.modelUtterance: Maybe next time.

dialogueNodes.text: …You're the one who confirms directions. Fine. But I'm not talking about feelings.
The bell ends lunch rush. Day three, and the cafeteria already feels less like enemy territory.

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Pameran klub yang riuh. Pemain harus memahami pengumuman aula dan membaca poster untuk menemukan klub yang cocok — dan menemukan stan Bridge Club yang kosong.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: Find Your Club — dengarkan pengumuman, baca tiga poster, temukan stan yang tidak dijaga.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Tidak ada risiko hubungan; episode ini menguji pemahaman fungsional.

dialogueNodes.text: SPEAKER: "Attention, all students. Club registration closes at one p.m. sharp. New clubs must submit a proposal form to the student affairs office by Wednesday. Booths are along the east wall." The hall is a jungle of banners.
Did you catch that? I was yelling at the basketball guys. What's the deal with new clubs?

choices.text: New clubs need a proposal form at the student affairs office by Wednesday. And booths close at one sharp.

feedback.message: Anda menangkap tindakan, tempat, dan tenggat — tiga hal yang selalu dicari dalam pengumuman.
feedback.modelUtterance: New clubs submit a proposal form by Wednesday; booths close at one sharp.

choices.text: Something about forms? And… one o'clock?

feedback.message: Setengah informasi bisa lebih berbahaya daripada tidak sama sekali. Kapan ragu — konfirmasi: "What form, and by when?"
feedback.modelUtterance: Wait — what form, and by when exactly?

choices.text: Yeah — clubs are cancelled this year.

feedback.message: Pengumuman tidak mengatakan itu. Menebak isi pengumuman menciptakan kabar bohong tanpa sengaja.
feedback.modelUtterance: I only caught part of it — let's ask at the desk.

dialogueNodes.text: Look at you, human notepad. Okay — posters. Find something you'd actually join. I'll be at the food row. Obviously.
"Something about forms." Incredible journalism. Let's read the posters — they'll have the details anyway.
Cancelled?! It literally said registration closes at one. Okay, no more announcements for you. Posters. Now.

Three posters. FILM CLUB: "Members only auditions, experience required, bring your portfolio." ROBOTICS: "Open to all — but meets Mondays AND Thursdays AND Saturdays." And a faded one: "GLOBAL COMMUNICATION CLUB — beginners welcome, no experience needed." Its booth is… empty.

choices.text: The faded one says beginners welcome — and nobody's even there. Why is the booth empty?

feedback.message: Anda membaca syarat ("beginners welcome") DAN memperhatikan kejanggalan. Membaca fungsional = menemukan yang penting, bukan membaca semua.
feedback.modelUtterance: It says beginners welcome — but the booth is empty.

choices.text: Film club it is — no requirements at all, right?

feedback.message: Poster film justru penuh syarat: members only, experience required, portfolio. Frasa pembatas adalah hal pertama yang harus dicari.
feedback.modelUtterance: Film club needs experience and a portfolio — that's not me yet.

dialogueNodes.text: You reread the film poster: members only… experience required… portfolio. Right. Maybe the faded poster deserves a second look — the one with the empty booth.
A dusty sign-up sheet. Zero names. A sticky note: "Back in 5 minutes (probably) — H." …H? The bell for last period rings. Mystery for tomorrow.

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Hana ternyata "H". Rencananya: menghidupkan kembali Global Communication Club. John diperkenalkan sebagai pembina. Aturan klub ditetapkan dan jadwal pertama dinegosiasikan.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: Revive the Club — sepakati jadwal mingguan dan pahami aturan klub dari John.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Episode pembentukan trio inti; pilihan memengaruhi antusiasme Hana dan kesediaan Ryo.

johnContent.learningContextId: Pertemuan pertama klub; John memperkenalkan aturan berbahasa Inggris di ruang klub.
johnContent.expectedStudentIntent: Bertanya tentang aturan klub dan menyepakati jadwal.
johnContent.tutorGoal: Menurunkan kecemasan berbicara; menegaskan bahwa bertanya dan salah adalah bagian latihan.
johnContent.contextualFollowUpIntent: Jika siswa diam atau samar, John bertanya ulang dengan pilihan konkret (Wednesday or Friday?).

dialogueNodes.text: Surprise. "H" is me. This is the Global Communication Club — or it was, before everyone graduated. I want to bring it back. And I want you in it. Both of you.
You dragged me from the workshop for an empty room. I have three questions: why me, what's the schedule, and is there a budget for equipment?

Good questions. I might be able to help with the answers. I'm John — I coach English communication here, and apparently, I'm your advisor now. One club rule from me: in this room, we try things in English. Badly is fine. Silently is not.
Any questions about the rule before we talk schedule?

choices.text: One thing — what does "advisor" mean, exactly? Like a teacher?

feedback.message: Anda memakai alat tanya-makna pada kesempatan nyata pertama. Persis seperti ini cara memperbesar kosakata.
feedback.modelUtterance: What does "advisor" mean, exactly?

choices.text: (Nod like you understood everything.)

feedback.message: John melihat anggukan kosong itu. Di ruang ini, pura-pura paham justru melanggar aturan #1.
feedback.modelUtterance: Sorry — what does "advisor" mean?

choices.text: I would like to request an official clarification regarding the terminology.

feedback.message: Berhasil — tetapi ini ruang klub, bukan sidang. "What does that mean?" cukup.
feedback.modelUtterance: What does that mean?

dialogueNodes.text: An advisor keeps you out of trouble and signs the paperwork. The club is yours — I just make sure it survives. Speaking of surviving: you need a weekly schedule for the proposal form.
That was a very confident nod for someone with question marks in their eyes. Advisor means: I sign papers, you run the club. Now — the schedule.
I have workshop Mondays and Thursdays. Hana has… whatever Hana has. Someone propose a day.

choices.text: What about Wednesdays? I'm free after three. Does that work for both of you?

feedback.message: Usulan + ketersediaan + cek kecocokan. Pola "Does that work for you?" dari pelajaran jadwal, dipakai sempurna.
feedback.modelUtterance: What about Wednesdays? Does that work for both of you?

choices.text: Whenever is fine. I don't mind. Anything.

feedback.message: "Terserah" terdengar fleksibel tetapi memindahkan seluruh beban keputusan ke orang lain. Kelompok butuh usulan konkret.
feedback.modelUtterance: How about Wednesday at three?

choices.text: I am free on yesterday afternoon.

feedback.message: "Yesterday" sudah lewat — jadwal memakai hari yang akan datang: "I'm free on Wednesday afternoons."
feedback.modelUtterance: I'm free on Wednesday afternoons.

dialogueNodes.text: "Whenever" is not a day of the week. Pick one. I schedule my life in thirty-minute blocks.
Yesterday afternoon. I'll inform the time machine club. …Wednesday. We meet Wednesday.
Wednesdays at three it is! Officially official. Now we just need three more signatures, or the school dissolves us before we even start. No pressure!

hotspots.label: Look at the old club photos

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Misi rekrutmen: menggambarkan klub secara menarik dan mengundang orang asing tanpa membuat mereka kabur. Penolakan pertama juga pelajaran.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: Three Signatures — dekati kandidat, deskripsikan klub, dan undang mereka. Minimal satu tanda tangan hari ini.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Cara Anda menangani penolakan Dewi memengaruhi apakah dia bergabung belakangan.

dialogueNodes.text: Target one: Dewi, 10-C. Loves drawing, hates crowds. Your mission: describe the club so it sounds like her kind of place. Go. I believe in you. Mostly.
Dewi looks up from her sketchbook, already suspicious.

choices.text: It's a small club — quiet room, world map on the door, right next to the library. We practice English through games and projects. Beginners welcome, no judging.

feedback.message: Deskripsi menyesuaikan pendengar: "small", "quiet", lokasi jelas — tepat untuk orang yang benci keramaian.
feedback.modelUtterance: It's a small, quiet club right next to the library — beginners welcome.

choices.text: It is a club. We do activities. It is good.

feedback.message: Bisa dipahami, tetapi tanpa detail tidak ada alasan untuk peduli. Deskripsi butuh ciri khas: tempat, suasana, kegiatan.
feedback.modelUtterance: It's a small club next to the library — we play English games and run one big project a semester.

choices.text: It's the most amazing incredible club in the history of Cakrawala!!

feedback.message: Untuk pendengar yang skeptis, hiperbola menurunkan kepercayaan. Fakta konkret lebih meyakinkan daripada superlatif.
feedback.modelUtterance: Honestly, it's tiny right now — that's kind of the charm.

dialogueNodes.text: DEWI: "Next to the library? …That's actually my hiding spot." She twirls her pencil. "What would I have to do?"
DEWI: "That describes literally every club." She goes back to sketching. You'll need to be more specific — what makes this club THIS club?
DEWI: "The most amazing club has zero members?" Ouch. Accurate, though. Honesty might work better here.
This is it — the actual invitation.

choices.text: Want to come see the room on Wednesday? Just look — no forms, no promises. Bring the sketchbook.

feedback.message: Undangan bertekanan rendah + detail personal ("bring the sketchbook"). Anda memudahkan Dewi berkata ya.
feedback.modelUtterance: Want to come see the room on Wednesday? No pressure.

choices.text: Sign here. We need three names by Friday or we're dissolved.

feedback.message: Jujur, tetapi Anda menjadikan masalah klub sebagai beban Dewi. Undangan menawarkan nilai, bukan menagih bantuan.
feedback.modelUtterance: Come see the room first — then decide.

dialogueNodes.text: DEWI: "…Fine. Wednesday. If anyone judges my English, I'm drawing them as a goblin." One signature down. Hana, watching from behind a pillar, gives a double thumbs-up with zero subtlety.
DEWI: "Sounds like your problem, not mine." Fair. Rejection one. Hana emerges from behind a pillar: "Okay, new plan — lead with the quiet room next time."
Two more targets this week. Then the form, then Ibu Ratna, then — officially a club. This is happening. I can feel it.

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Ujian dari Ibu Ratna: klub baru harus menjadi tuan rumah siswa pertukaran dari Singapura, Maya. Membaca emailnya dan membalas dengan register yang tepat menentukan kesan pertama klub.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: Host Duty — baca email Maya, susun balasan, dan sepakati jadwal kunjungan.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Balasan yang jelas dan hangat membuat kedatangan Maya di Episode 8 lebih bersahabat.

dialogueNodes.text: Congratulations on your provisional status. Now, your first duty. An exchange student from Singapore visits next Friday. Your club will host her campus tour. Consider it… a test. Her email is on the desk. Do reply properly.

FROM: Maya Chen. "Hi! I'm Maya — I'll be visiting Cakrawala on Friday. Could you let me know what time works best, and whether I should prepare anything? Also, slightly important: I'm vegetarian, in case lunch is involved. Can't wait! — M." Hana slides you the keyboard: "You write it. I'll supervise. Supervising is my talent."
Okay — she asked three things. Time, preparation, and the lunch thing. Your reply?

choices.text: "Hi Maya! Friday at 9 a.m. works great — we'll meet you at the front gate. No need to prepare anything, just comfy shoes. And noted on the vegetarian lunch, we know the right stall. See you Friday!"

feedback.message: Tiga pertanyaan, tiga jawaban, nada hangat, dan detail praktis ("comfy shoes"). Balasan yang membuat tamu tenang.
feedback.modelUtterance: Friday at 9 works great — we'll meet you at the front gate.

choices.text: "Hi Maya. Friday is OK. See you."

feedback.message: Dua pertanyaan Maya tidak terjawab: persiapan dan makan siang vegetarian. Balasan yang baik menjawab SEMUA yang ditanya.
feedback.modelUtterance: Friday at 9 works — nothing to prepare, and we've noted the vegetarian lunch.

choices.text: "Dear Honored Miss Chen, We hereby confirm receipt of your correspondence regarding the forthcoming visitation…"

feedback.message: Maya seumuran Anda dan emailnya santai. Cermin nada lawan bicara: ramah, jelas, tidak kaku.
feedback.modelUtterance: Hi Maya! Friday at 9 works great.

dialogueNodes.text: Send it. SEND IT. Okay — she replied already: "Perfect, front gate at 9! ps: comfy shoes acquired." I like her. Now we just need a tour plan that doesn't embarrass us.
Wait wait — she asked about preparing AND the vegetarian thing. If she ends up hungry in front of Ibu Ratna, we're a cautionary tale. Fix it, add the missing answers.
"Honored Miss Chen"?! She's sixteen, not a duchess! Rewrite it like a human — friendly, short, all three answers.

Tour plan: gate at 9, classrooms, then Ryo's workshop — he's weirdly excited, he rehearsed a demo — cafeteria at 12 for the vegetarian-friendly stall, library after. I'll print the schedule. This is going to work. Right? Right.

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Set piece musim ini: memandu Maya keliling kampus. Bahasa arah, deskripsi, dan prosedur dari seluruh semester dipakai dengan taruhan nyata — di depan tamu dan Ibu Ratna. Beat berlabel RECALL menghasilkan bukti retrieval.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: The Grand Tour — pandu Maya dari gerbang ke bengkel media tanpa membuatnya tersesat, bosan, atau lapar.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Tur yang baik menaikkan kepercayaan Maya dan Ryo. Ini episode recall utama Season 1.

dialogueNodes.text: Front gate, nine sharp, comfy shoes as promised! Okay, tour guide — I'm all yours. Where are we headed first, and how do we get there?
(She unfolds a tiny map of the campus she printed herself.) Show off your campus knowledge!

choices.text: We'll go straight past the science labs, turn right at the stairs, and the media workshop is the third door on the left — you can't miss it, the door's covered in film posters.

feedback.message: Bahasa arah dari awal semester — kini Anda yang memberi arah, bukan meminta. Retrieval sempurna: urutan, belokan, ciri khas.
feedback.modelUtterance: Straight past the labs, right at the stairs, third door on the left — you can't miss it.

choices.text: It's… that way. Somewhere over there. We'll find it.

feedback.message: "Somewhere over there" adalah kalimat yang membuat Anda tersesat di minggu pertama. Ingat polanya: past — turn — on your left.
feedback.modelUtterance: Straight past the labs, right at the stairs, third door on the left.

choices.text: We go under the labs and turn at up the stairs.

feedback.message: Preposisi arah: "past the labs" (melewati), "at the stairs" (di tangga). "Under" berarti di bawah gedung — semoga tidak.
feedback.modelUtterance: We go past the labs and turn right at the stairs.

dialogueNodes.text: Film posters on the door — there it is! You give better directions than my map app. Ooh, and this must be the famous workshop.
(Twenty minutes and one wrong corridor later…) Found it! No thanks to your directions, tour guide. Good thing your school has helpful wall maps.
Welcome to the workshop. I prepared a demonstration: how we set up a camera slider. But my English gets… compressed when I explain. Back me up if I go too fast.
"Rail on tripod. Lock. Camera on plate. Balance. Test slide. Done." (Maya blinks. That was six words per step.)

choices.text: Let me translate Ryo-speak: first, mount the rail on the tripod and make sure it's locked. Then attach the camera to the plate and check the balance. Finally, do one slow test slide before recording.

feedback.message: Bahasa prosedur lengkap: urutan (first/then/finally) + peringatan (make sure). Anda mengubah telegraf Ryo menjadi penjelasan yang bisa diikuti.
feedback.modelUtterance: First, mount the rail and make sure it's locked. Then attach the camera. Finally, do a test slide.

choices.text: Yes. Rail. Lock. Camera. Done.

feedback.message: Sekarang ada dua orang berbahasa telegraf. Tamu butuh langkah berurutan dengan penanda: first, then, make sure, finally.
feedback.modelUtterance: First, mount the rail and lock it. Then attach the camera and check the balance.

dialogueNodes.text: Okay, that I understood! Can I try the test slide? (Ryo, quietly, to you:) "…Good translation." — From Ryo, that's a medal.
I'm going to nod like I got that, but between us — I did not get that. Show me slowly? (You walk her through it step by step, properly this time.)
Vegetarian lunch, a workshop demo, and zero times lost — okay, one time. Cakrawala passes. And your club? I'm telling my school about you. You three are kind of great.

lessonBlocks.sceneIntentId: Finale Season 1. Maya pulang; klub resmi berdiri. Di pertemuan resmi pertama, pemain menceritakan kisah satu semester — recount + storytelling dengan seluruh ungkapan musim ini.
lessonBlocks.questIntentId: Quest: First Official Meeting — ceritakan kisah semester ini di depan klub. Buka dengan hook, jaga urutan, tutup dengan makna.
lessonBlocks.relationshipNotes: Finale membayar semua nilai hubungan: reaksi Hana, Ryo, dan Dewi berbeda sesuai perjalanan Anda.

dialogueNodes.text: Item one on the agenda of the OFFICIALLY OFFICIAL Global Communication Club: Maya's school wants a partnership next year. Item two: Ibu Ratna smiled. Witnesses exist. Item three — the story. New tradition: every semester ends with someone telling our story. Guess who's up.
Remember: a story isn't a report. Hook us, walk us through it, land somewhere that matters. The room is yours.
Dewi's pencil hovers over her sketchbook. Ryo sets a timer — of course he does. How do you begin?

choices.text: Okay, so this happened on my very first morning — I got lost, twice, and the guy who saved me refused to use full sentences.

feedback.message: Hook sempurna: janji cerita + humor + karakter. Semua kepala terangkat.
feedback.modelUtterance: Okay, so this happened on my very first morning…

choices.text: This semester, several events occurred. I will list them chronologically.

feedback.message: Itu pembukaan laporan, bukan cerita. "You won't believe…" atau "Okay, so this happened…" mengundang pendengar masuk.
feedback.modelUtterance: You won't believe how this semester started.

dialogueNodes.text: You build it: at first, a stranger at the gate who talked too fast and became a best friend. Then an empty clubroom that smelled like dust and possibility. And guess what — the scary silent guy? He rehearsed a camera demo for a guest he'd never met. Dewi is drawing furiously. Even Ryo's timer is forgotten.

"Event one: registration. Event two: recruitment." Ryo checks his timer. Dewi draws a small sleeping goblin. John catches your eye and mouths: at first… then… You take a breath and switch gears — and the room comes back.
The ending is yours.

choices.text: And that's how an empty room became the best part of my week. In the end, I didn't just get used to this school — this school got used to us.

feedback.message: "And that's how…" + "in the end" + callback ke "getting used to it" dari Episode 1. Penutup yang mengikat seluruh musim.
feedback.modelUtterance: And that's how an empty room became the best part of my week.

choices.text: So. Yeah. That's it. The end.

feedback.message: Cerita sebagus itu layak mendarat, bukan jatuh. Satu kalimat makna — "and that's how…" — mengubah segalanya.
feedback.modelUtterance: And that's how three strangers became a club.

dialogueNodes.text: (Suspiciously shiny eyes.) Okay wow. New rule: tissues in the club budget. Ryo — time? RYO: "Four minutes twenty. …Worth every second." Semester one: complete.
"That's it"?! You can't just— okay fine, the middle was SO good you're forgiven. Next semester: stick the landing. Semester one: complete anyway.

hotspots.label: Look at the framed club charter
